hypothesis: Applying class-balancing strategies during training—specifically oversampling rare reaction templates or undersampling frequent ones to create a more uniform template frequency distribution—will disproportionately improve top-k accuracy for rare templates (frequency 1–10) without substantially degrading accuracy for common templates (frequency >10,001), thereby reducing the performance disparity between rare and frequent templates observed across all three architectures while simultaneously improving out-of-distribution generalization to unseen templates in the Pistachio OOD test set.
